crime.value: Murder of lawyer Carly on Judd Street, stabbed with a knife; question: why was she lured there by a fake shortcut sign?
crime.quote: They had just been told that their mom, Carly, was murdered. Carly was a lawyer and often had to work late.

victim.value: Carly, a lawyer who worked late hours, making her vulnerable to being lured to dangerous locations
victim.quote: Carly was a lawyer and often had to work late.

investigator.value: Kristy, the victim's daughter with perfect memory who notices evidence the police miss
investigator.quote: Kristy walked over to a police man... 'I think I found some evidence,' Kristy replied, pointing at the sign.

red_herring.value: Fake shortcut sign planted with sharpie to misdirect investigation
red_herring.quote: Was totally fake and was with written with sharpie, Kristy analyzed

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence shown to reader through protagonist's observations - fake sign and knife examination
clue_fairness.quote: The police officers haven't even noticed the sign. They were too busy analyzing the dead body

social_world.value: Legal profession hierarchy - both parents lawyers, father takes over mother's practice
social_world.quote: Since Dad was a lawyer too, he worked late. At first he was doctor but he took over Mom's job

reveal_mechanic.value: Protagonist's perfect memory ability used to analyze physical evidence and recognize inconsistencies
reveal_mechanic.quote: A person who remebers everything? ...remembering the time she used sharpie to make her poster

media_and_audience.value: Crime scene recognized from news coverage, establishing public awareness of local murders
media_and_audience.quote: A lot of people have been murdered here, Kristy thought, recognizing the street from the news

investigator_wound.value: Protagonist investigating her own mother's murder while maintaining emotional distance
investigator_wound.quote: Kristy wanted to go comfort them, but first she had to tell the police the pure evidence
